summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on June 8, 2021. Key highlights included recognition of Tyler and Tasha Bailey of Wage War Apparel for their community contributions including designing a new police patch and township logo, the appointment of Nick Colliver as a full-time police officer, approval to sell a 1998 Sutton fire engine to Scott Township for $8,500, approval of a HealthSource easement exchange for a CDBG turn lane project, rescheduling the August meeting to avoid conflicting with National Night Out, and appointment of Gary Theodore as BBA alternate.
decisions: Approved appointment of Nick Colliver as full-time police officer at $67,237.28 salary effective June 28, 2021 with one-year probationary period
Approved sale of 1998 Sutton fire engine to Scott Township (Adams County) for $8,500
Approved in concept the expansion of HealthSource entrance/exit on Charles Snyder Road in exchange for easement for 2021 CDBG turn lane widening project
Approved moving August special meeting from August 3rd back to regular meeting date of August 10th at 7pm to avoid conflict with National Night Out
Appointed Gary Theodore as BBA alternate for remainder of year with term expiring December 31, 2022
action_items: Cheryl to coordinate a photo day with new police car, fire truck, and service truck in front of building with Doug doing drone photography
Chief Rose to follow up on speeding enforcement on Goshen Road
Administrator Pegram to continue work on 2022 tax budget preparation for July
Choice One Engineering designing the CDBG turn lane widening project on Charles Snyder Road
Planning for kickball game with police officers in August
key_amounts: $67,237.28 - starting salary for new police officer Nick Colliver
$8,500 - sale price for 1998 Sutton fire engine to Scott Township
$17,000 - original purchase price of the fire engine
$12,000 - Scott Township's initial offer for fire engine
notable_topics: Recognition of Tyler and Tasha Bailey (Wage War Apparel) for designing new police patch and township logo
50th anniversary of Goshen Township Police Department establishment
New township logo with message 'Love Where You Live'
Appointment of full-time police officer Nick Colliver
Sale of surplus 1998 fire engine to Scott Township
HealthSource easement exchange for CDBG turn lane widening on Charles Snyder Road
Rescheduling August meeting to avoid National Night Out conflict
Memorial Day event and community cleanup day recap
Executive session followed by BBA alternate appointment
Speeding enforcement warning on Goshen Road
attendees: John Bentine (Vice President, Board of Education)
notes: The transcript contains significant garbled and repeated text, likely due to automated transcription errors. The meeting marked a return to more normal in-person meetings after COVID restrictions. An executive session was held from approximately 7:47 p.m. to 8:20 p.m. The new police patch was noted as coinciding with the 50th anniversary of the Goshen Township Police Department. A viral video of a police officer playing kickball was mentioned.
title: BOT MTG 6.8.21
